title: Bedford-Pine urban renewal area : environmental impact statement.

entities.entity: Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: On October 15, 1973, the bids were opened and on December 12, 1973, Park Central Communities was conditionally awarded an option to purchase all 78 acres for a disposition price of approximately 11 million dollars.
entities.stance_basis: Authority advertised and conditionally awarded sale of 78 acres, advancing the project

entities.role: conditional purchaser / redeveloper of 78-acre CDP tract
entities.quote: On October 15, 1973, the bids were opened and on December 12, 1973, Park Central Communities was conditionally awarded an option to purchase all 78 acres for a disposition price of approximately 11 million dollars.
entities.stance_basis: Submitted winning bid and accepted conditional option to purchase and develop the 78-acre CDP tract

entities.quote: Federal, State, and Local Agencies And Affected Citizen Groups from which Comments are being Requested: ... Bedford-Pine Project Area Committee.
entities.stance_basis: Listed as a group from which comments are being requested; CDP must be approved by the Project Area Committee

entities.quote: Federal, State, and Local Agencies And Affected Citizen Groups from which Comments are being Requested: ... Atlanta Audubon Society, Georgia Conservancy, and Georgia Wildlife Federation
entities.stance_basis: Listed as a group from which comments are being requested

entities.quote: Federal, State, and Local Agencies And Affected Citizen Groups from which Comments are being Requested: ... Atlanta Audubon Society, Georgia Conservancy, and Georgia Wildlife Federation
entities.stance_basis: Listed as a group from which comments are being requested

entities.quote: Federal, State, and Local Agencies And Affected Citizen Groups from which Comments are being Requested: ... Atlanta Audubon Society, Georgia Conservancy, and Georgia Wildlife Federation
entities.stance_basis: Listed as a group from which comments are being requested

entities.entity: U. S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
entities.quote: The revision was given a Special Environmental Clearance on November 20, 1973, (see Appendix 1) with the finding that an Environmental Impact Statement would be necessary prior to the revision because of significance of impact by such a Comprehensive Development Plan in the area.
entities.stance_basis: HUD prepared the EIS and granted special environmental clearances advancing the project

entities.role: staff nurse, Fulton County Health Department Northeast Branch
entities.quote: According to Mrs. Arnold, a staff nurse, the following health services are provided at the center, all at no cost to the patient except for those of the Maternal Health Clinic. Mrs. Arnold also stated that the Well Baby Clinic is well used, and the Family Planning Clinic receives very good community support.
entities.stance_basis: Provided factual information about health services; no stance on the project taken

entities.role: cooperating agency / verifier of redeveloper calculations
entities.quote: Source: Redeveloper Calculations, verified by Atlanta Housing Authority and HUD.
entities.stance_basis: verified redeveloper noise penetration calculations, indicating acceptance of methodology

entities.quote: Source: Redeveloper Calculations, verified by Atlanta Housing Authority and HUD.
entities.stance_basis: verified redeveloper calculations for noise penetration and open space/population tables

entities.quote: According to Dr. Womack of APS, there is a decline in school enrollment on all levels, (eg., elementary, middle, and high); therefore the excess 103 students would not be a problem.
entities.stance_basis: assessed that projected excess middle school demand would not be a problem given enrollment decline trends

entities.role: school system providing enrollment projections and capacity assessments
entities.quote: The Atlanta Public School System estimates the existing capacity of Walden to be 852 students.
entities.stance_basis: provided capacity estimates for Walden Middle School relevant to project impact assessment

entities.quote: the Atlanta Public Library staff expressed interest in leasing space within the completed project to provide an expanded branch library.
entities.stance_basis: expressed interest in leasing space within the project to expand library services

entities.quote: The Fire Department believes it has adequate capacity to meet fire protection needs in the area.
entities.stance_basis: affirmed adequate capacity and willingness to upgrade service levels as needed for the project

entities.role: representative of Community Coordinated Child Care Program
entities.quote: Jackqueline T. Cook of the Community Coordinated Child Care Program recommended that a logical percentage distribution of service by age groups would be:
entities.stance_basis: provided technical recommendations on age-group distribution for day care demand planning

entities.role: cooperating agency on open space standards
entities.quote: Any evaluation of how Park Central's proposed project will affect the open space environment described in Section F is based upon the open space standards established specifically for the project by the redeveloper and agreed upon by the Atlanta Housing Authority, Project Area Committee, and City Planning Department.
entities.stance_basis: agreed upon open space standards for the project

entities.role: citizen participation body / project oversight committee
entities.quote: Any evaluation of how Park Central's proposed project will affect the open space environment described in Section F is based upon the open space standards established specifically for the project by the redeveloper and agreed upon by the Atlanta Housing Authority, Project Area Committee, and City Planning Department.
entities.stance_basis: agreed upon open space standards for the project

entities.quote: Any evaluation of how Park Central's proposed project will affect the open space environment described in Section F is based upon the open space standards established specifically for the project by the redeveloper and agreed upon by the Atlanta Housing Authority, Project Area Committee, and City Planning Department.
entities.stance_basis: agreed upon open space standards for the project

entities.quote: the Parks and Recreation Department has said that the recreation center now under construction is meant to serve the existing community and its expanded service area.
entities.stance_basis: indicated existing recreation center may not be adequate to serve new project demand, raising concern without opposing

entities.role: representative of Northeast Branch Office, Fulton County Health Department
entities.quote: Mrs. Arnold of the Northeast Branch Office of the Fulton County Health Department indicated that facilities were presently not being used to capacity, and that they were developing a mechanism to ensure that area residents become more knowledgeable about their services.
entities.stance_basis: indicated health facilities had capacity and were developing outreach mechanisms for project area residents

entities.entity: Educational Facilities Planning Center, College of Education, University of Georgia
entities.quote: An Educational facilities survey prepared by the Educational Facilities Planning Center, College of Education, University of Georgia indicated that Walden could possibly house 996 students.
entities.stance_basis: provided higher capacity estimate for Walden Middle School, suggesting project demand could be met

entities.role: citizen participation body for Bedford-Pine redevelopment area
entities.quote: In order for the Project Area Committee to approve the Comprehensive Development Plan, the plan must have adequate controls that will benefit existing residents of the area.
entities.stance_basis: approval of Comprehensive Development Plan conditioned on adequate controls benefiting existing residents

entities.entity: Atlanta Housing Authority Board of Commissioners
entities.role: approving authority for Comprehensive Development Plan
entities.quote: the land cannot be conveyed without approval of the Comprehensive Development Plan by the Atlanta Housing Authority Board of Commissioners, the council of the City of Atlanta, the Department of Housing and Urban Development, and the Project Area Committee.
entities.stance_basis: identified as required approving authority for land conveyance under the project

entities.entity: council of the City of Atlanta
entities.role: approving authority for Comprehensive Development Plan
entities.quote: the land cannot be conveyed without approval of the Comprehensive Development Plan by the Atlanta Housing Authority Board of Commissioners, the council of the City of Atlanta, the Department of Housing and Urban Development, and the Project Area Committee.
entities.stance_basis: identified as required approving authority for land conveyance under the project

entities.entity: Department of Housing and Urban Development
entities.role: federal approving authority for Comprehensive Development Plan
entities.quote: the land cannot be conveyed without approval of the Comprehensive Development Plan by the Atlanta Housing Authority Board of Commissioners, the council of the City of Atlanta, the Department of Housing and Urban Development, and the Project Area Committee.
entities.stance_basis: identified as required approving authority for land conveyance under the project

entities.role: Vice President, Park Central Communities, Inc.
entities.quote: SOURCE: Land Development Analysts' interview with Joseph G. Martin, Jr., Vice President, Park Central Communities, Inc., May 17, 1974, for number of households by income group.
entities.stance_basis: provided household income data supporting commercial demand analysis for the project

entities.quote: The Atlanta Housing Authority will: (1) utilize the generous monetary benefits provided by the Uniform Relocation Act, (2) make every effort, legally possible, to relocate any residents who so desire back into the Bedford-Pine renewal area, and (3) creating design standards to provide for new housing within the project which clearly answers the social and recreation needs of the area residents.
entities.stance_basis: commits to implementing relocation and redevelopment per the proposed action

entities.quote: After weighing all environmental impacts, HUD decides it appropriate and feasible to proceed with Design Alternative C as discussed on page 387 and Section I (c)(3).
entities.stance_basis: recommends proceeding with Design Alternative C (proposed action)

entities.quote: ARC requests that immediate contact be made with ARC's Chief of Transportation Planning, Mr. Joel Stone, and that steps be taken to incorporate the proposed improvements into the transportation planning process for the Atlanta Region.
entities.stance_basis: requests coordination of street improvements with regional transportation planning process before proceeding

entities.quote: Whether the Draft Environmental Impact Statement actually does evaluate all the ramifications of those plans is questionable. We think it does not, and that it requires further revision in order to do so.
entities.stance_basis: finds Draft EIS deficient and requires further revision before acceptance

entities.quote: We believe that the implication that MARTA will construct a rubber-tired, fixed guideway system between the Civic Center and Civic Center Station is premature.
entities.stance_basis: objects to specific implication in Draft EIS regarding MARTA system construction

entities.quote: The status of the various phases of Project Rehab has changed. For instance, the reservation of funds for Parkway/Boulevard Phase III has now been cancelled.
entities.stance_basis: requests corrections and clarifications to Draft EIS to accurately reflect redeveloper commitments and current status

entities.quote: It is suggested that the recommended transportation improvements (pages 220-225 and page 382) be coordinated with the Atlanta Regional Commission (ARC) concerning inclusion of suggested improvements in the Atlanta Metro Transportation Improvement Program (TIP).
entities.stance_basis: suggests coordination conditions for transportation improvements before proceeding

entities.quote: We have reviewed the Draft Environmental Impact Statement for Bedford-Pine Urban Renewal Area in Atlanta, Georgia, and believe it could be improved by giving additional details. Details for erosion control measures to be used should be more explicit.
entities.stance_basis: requests additional detail on erosion control and air quality analysis before EIS is adequate

entities.quote: We note major deficiencies in your presentation of the impact of the proposed project on "Energy" (Section II.F(1)(d), for there is no mention of any quantitative energy analysis.
entities.stance_basis: identifies major deficiencies in energy analysis and requires quantitative energy assessment in final EIS

entities.quote: The statement does not adequately identify cultural resources, nor does it adequately assess the project's potential environmental impacts on these resources.
entities.stance_basis: finds EIS inadequate regarding cultural resource identification and assessment, requires additional studies

entities.entity: Council of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: That the Comprehensive Development Plans, as attached hereto, having been duly received and considered, are hereby approved
entities.stance_basis: Formally approved the Comprehensive Development Plans and pledged cooperation in carrying them out

entities.entity: Mayor of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: APPROVED by the Mayor Mar. 25, 1975
entities.stance_basis: Formally approved the Comprehensive Development Plans on March 25, 1975

entities.entity: Board of Commissioners of The Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.role: Governing board of the Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: That the Comprehensive Development Plan attached hereto, pertaining to Parcels 16N, 16S, and (42, 44 and 45), and the Comprehensive Development Plan attached hereto, pertaining to Parcels 17, 18, 19, 57 and 58 in the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Area, Project-Georgia A-2-1, are hereby approved.
entities.stance_basis: Unanimously adopted resolution approving Comprehensive Development Plans and accepting Park Central Communities proposals

entities.quote: Submitted By Park Central Communities, Inc., With Concurrence By The Bedford-Pine Project Area Committee On February 26, 1975
entities.stance_basis: Submitted the Comprehensive Development Plans for the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Area parcels

entities.quote: Submitted By Park Central Communities, Inc., With Concurrence By The Bedford-Pine Project Area Committee On February 26, 1975
entities.stance_basis: Concurred with the submission of the Comprehensive Development Plans on February 26, 1975

entities.entity: Director of Redevelopment and the staff of the Housing Authority
entities.role: Staff of the Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: the Director of Redevelopment and the staff of the Housing Authority recommend that the said Comprehensive Development Plans be approved by the Board of Commissioners and that the proposals of said redeveloper be accepted
entities.stance_basis: Formally recommended approval of the Comprehensive Development Plans and acceptance of the redeveloper's proposals

entities.quote: the City of Atlanta guarantees that it will use every resource at its command to assure that the Redeveloper fulfills this obligation.
entities.stance_basis: Guaranteed use of all resources to ensure the redeveloper fulfills assisted housing obligations

entities.role: Commissioner, Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: Upon motion made by Mrs. Louise Watley and seconded by Mr. Benjamin W. Spaulding, the following resolution was unanimously adopted
entities.stance_basis: Made the motion to adopt the resolution approving the Comprehensive Development Plans

entities.role: Commissioner, Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: Upon motion made by Mrs. Louise Watley and seconded by Mr. Benjamin W. Spaulding, the following resolution was unanimously adopted
entities.stance_basis: Seconded the motion to adopt the resolution approving the Comprehensive Development Plans

entities.role: Counsel for The Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: The Plan in form and substance is in accordance with Chapter 69-11, Georgia Code Annotated. The Plan has been duly approved by the Mayor and City Council of the City of Atlanta and by the Board of Commissioners of the Local Public Agency, and as approved is valid, meeting all the requirements of State and local law and of Title I of the Housing Act of 1949, as amended.
entities.stance_basis: Legal counsel opines the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Plan is valid and meets all legal requirements

entities.entity: Mayor and City Council of the City of Atlanta
entities.role: Governing body of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: The Plan has been duly approved by the Mayor and City Council of the City of Atlanta and by the Board of Commissioners of the Local Public Agency, and as approved is valid, meeting all the requirements of State and local law and of Title I of the Housing Act of 1949, as amended.
entities.stance_basis: Formally approved the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Plan

entities.entity: The Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta, Georgia
entities.quote: The Plan has been duly approved by the Mayor and City Council of the City of Atlanta and by the Board of Commissioners of the Local Public Agency, and as approved is valid, meeting all the requirements of State and local law and of Title I of the Housing Act of 1949, as amended.
entities.stance_basis: Board of Commissioners approved the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Plan

entities.role: Plaintiff, Civil Action No. 16901, U.S. District Court, Northern District of Georgia
entities.quote: The case of Freeman, et al. v. The housing Authority of the City of Atlanta, Caorgia, Civil Action No. 16901, United States District Court, Northern District of Georgia, has a potential affect upon the Local Public Agency's urban renewal activities in the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Area. It involves a number of issues, primarily the alleged non-compliance with the National Environmental Policies Act, and seeks injunctive relief against various activities in that area.
entities.stance_basis: Filed suit alleging non-compliance with NEPA and seeking injunctive relief against urban renewal activities

entities.quote: Redeveloper - Park Central Communities, Inc., (PCC), its successor, assigns, agents, or any other entity accepting obligations in behalf of PCC.
entities.stance_basis: Designated Redeveloper responsible for implementing the Comprehensive Development Plan

entities.quote: Seek compliance by the Metropolitan Atlanta Rapid Transit Authority in its commitment to provide a people-mover link from the rail rapid transit line to the Civic Center.
entities.stance_basis: Has made a commitment to provide a people-mover link supporting the redevelopment plan

entities.quote: Widen Pine Street to four moving lanes between Piedmont Avenue and the off-ramp from the Downtown Connector and work with the Georgia Department of Transportation (DOT) to improve the design of this off-ramp.
entities.stance_basis: Identified as a partner to work with on road improvements associated with the redevelopment

entities.entity: Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta, Georgia
entities.quote: NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE BOARD OF COMMISSIONERS OF THE HOUSING AUTHORITY OF THE CITY OF ATLANTA, GEORGIA: 1. That the Executive Director of the Local Public Agency is hereby authorized and directed to execute and to file any necessary revisions to the agency's previously filed application with the Department as a result of this amendment to the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Plan
entities.stance_basis: Board resolution authorizing execution of amended Urban Redevelopment Plan and all associated actions

entities.entity: City Council of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: following action by the Development Committee, the City Council of the City of Atlanta, Georgia, did on March 17, 1975 adopt a resolution approving said Urban Redevelopment Plan, as amended, and making, with respect to the Area, the findings and determinations prescribed in Section 69-1119 of the Georgia Code Annotated
entities.stance_basis: Adopted resolution approving the amended Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Plan

entities.entity: Development Committee of the City Council of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: the Development Committee of the City Council of the City of Atlanta, Georgia, has, after due notice and in accordance with Section 69-1106 of the Georgia Code Annotated, presented the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Plan, as amended, at a public hearing on March 14, 1975
entities.stance_basis: Presented the amended Urban Redevelopment Plan at public hearing, facilitating its approval

entities.quote: Approving the Comprehensive Development Plans for Parcels 16N, 16S, 17, 18, 19, (42, 44 and 45) 57 and 58 in the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Area, Project Georgia A-2-1, and accepting the proposals of Park Central Communities, Inc., to purchase and redevelop said parcels.
entities.stance_basis: Submitted proposals to purchase and redevelop parcels in the Bedford-Pine Urban Redevelopment Area

entities.entity: City of Atlanta Bureau of Planning
entities.quote: The planning proposals contained in this Plan as they relate to land uses, thoroughfare, street changes, and other redevelopment actions contemplated were reviewed and approved by the City of Atlanta Bureau of Planning.
entities.stance_basis: Reviewed and approved the planning proposals contained in the Urban Redevelopment Plan

entities.role: Commissioner, Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: We, the undersigned Commissioners of The Housing Authority of The City of Atlanta, Georgia, do hereby accept service of the NOTICE OF SPECIAL MEETING waiving any and all irregularities in such service and in such Notice and Consent and agree that the said Commissioners of said Authority shall meet at the time and place named in such Notice and for the purpose stated therein.
entities.stance_basis: Signed waiver consenting to special meeting to approve the amended Urban Redevelopment Plan

entities.role: Commissioner, Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: We, the undersigned Commissioners of The Housing Authority of The City of Atlanta, Georgia, do hereby accept service of the NOTICE OF SPECIAL MEETING waiving any and all irregularities in such service and in such Notice and Consent and agree that the said Commissioners of said Authority shall meet at the time and place named in such Notice and for the purpose stated therein.
entities.stance_basis: Signed waiver consenting to special meeting to approve the amended Urban Redevelopment Plan

entities.role: Commissioner, Housing Authority of the City of Atlanta
entities.quote: We, the undersigned Commissioners of The Housing Authority of The City of Atlanta, Georgia, do hereby accept service of the NOTICE OF SPECIAL MEETING waiving any and all irregularities in such service and in such Notice and Consent and agree that the said Commissioners of said Authority shall meet at the time and place named in such Notice and for the purpose stated therein.
entities.stance_basis: Signed waiver consenting to special meeting to approve the amended Urban Redevelopment Plan

entities.role: public school system serving Bedford-Pine community
entities.quote: While there are many factors which contribute to Bedford-Pine schools' low achievement rating, socio-economic factors are considered by Atlanta Public School officials as being foremost.
entities.stance_basis: APS attributes low achievement to socio-economic factors rather than school quality deficiencies

entities.role: consultant preparing institutional development plan for Bedford-Pine
entities.quote: We recommend that in the short range the Community Development Corporation with assistance from the developer establish a mechanism to meet these objectives.
entities.stance_basis: recommends comprehensive social service programs conditional on developer and CDC funding commitments

entities.quote: Residents of the Bedford-Pine Community have indicated strong interest in this approach to delivering Health Services.
entities.stance_basis: expressed strong interest in Health Maintenance Operation (HMO) approach

entities.quote: The Atlanta Police Department is presently in the implementation phase of instituting several community assistance programs in the area of security.
entities.stance_basis: actively implementing THOR community security assistance programs in the Bedford-Pine area

entities.role: Director of Howard High School community program
entities.quote: The Director of Howard's program is very enthusiastic about having the community utilize the services Howard provides, as well as adding programs based on the desires of the community.
entities.stance_basis: enthusiastic support for expanding community use of Howard High School programs

entities.entity: Area Target Director, Department of Human Resources
entities.role: State of Georgia Department of Human Resources official
entities.quote: The Area Target Director, Department of Human Resources, indicated that the $1,600 maximum figure could possibly be increased under special program conditions.
entities.stance_basis: provided informational clarification on per-child cost maximums without taking a position on the project

entities.entity: State of Georgia, Department of Human Resources
entities.role: state agency administering day care funding
entities.quote: According to sources at the office of contract services, State Department of Human Resources, an organization such as a Community Development Corporation can provide day care services for profit and non-profit purposes under one roof.
entities.stance_basis: confirmed eligibility of CDC structure for day care services without endorsing or opposing the project

entities.entity: Atlanta University Center, School for Community Services
entities.role: technical assistance provider for School Community Councils
entities.quote: Atlanta University Center, School for Community Services, provides technical assistance in organizing councils.
entities.stance_basis: actively providing technical assistance to organize School Community Councils in Bedford-Pine

entities.quote: Existing community organizations (PAC, EOA East Central Neighborhood office) would be extensively involved in this development process.
entities.stance_basis: designated as extensively involved in developing the community forum/association for Bedford-Pine

entities.quote: Existing community organizations (PAC, EOA East Central Neighborhood office) would be extensively involved in this development process.
entities.stance_basis: designated as extensively involved in developing the community forum/association for Bedford-Pine

entities.role: existing Bedford-Pine on-site and off-site residents
entities.quote: Present area residents have often stated that the police presence and response to calls is inadequate and on occasion non-existent.
entities.stance_basis: opposed to inadequate police protection in the Bedford-Pine area

entities.quote: The major comments of the Atlanta Regional Commission's staff are are follows: The Draft EIS for the Bedford-Pine Project shows recommended street widenings and other roadway improvements proposed as part of the project construction. These improvements, which are listed below, have not been coordinated with the Atlanta Region transportation planning process.
entities.stance_basis: requests coordination of proposed street improvements with regional transportation planning process before proceeding

entities.quote: If the City of Atlanta and HUD wish to pursue these proposed improvements, the staff of the Atlanta Regional Commission would request that an immediate contact be made with ARC's Chief of Transportation Planning, Mr. Joel Stone, at the above address and that steps be taken to incorporate the proposed improvements into the transportation planning process for the Atlanta Region.
entities.stance_basis: conditions support on incorporation of improvements into regional transportation planning process

entities.quote: the major request for change from these agencies is in the MARTA response wherein they state that they believe the implication that MARTA will construct a rubber-tired, fixed guideway system between the Civic Center and Civic Center Station is premature.
entities.stance_basis: objects to characterization that MARTA will construct a specific transit system as premature

entities.role: Manager of Urban Planning, Metropolitan Atlanta Rapid Transit Authority
entities.quote: We believe that the implication that MARTA will construct a rubber-tired, fixed guideway system between the Civic Center and Civic Center Station is premature.
entities.stance_basis: directly states that the EIS implication about MARTA constructing a specific transit system is premature

entities.role: Director, Bureau of Planning, City of Atlanta
entities.quote: The Bureau of Planning recommends approval of these program applications.
entities.stance_basis: recommends approval of the Bedford Pine Renewal Area EIS program application

entities.quote: DeKalb County is in general agreement that the central city needs a greater diversity of housing. We have no information on this EIS and have no specific comments.
entities.stance_basis: general agreement with housing diversity goal but no specific comments on the EIS

entities.role: Special Projects Director, DeKalb County Planning Department
entities.quote: DeKalb County is in general agreement that the central city needs a greater diversity of housing. We have no information on this EIS and have no specific comments.
entities.stance_basis: general agreement with housing diversity goal but no specific comments on the EIS

entities.quote: We note major deficiencies in your presentation of the impact of the proposed project on "Energy" (Section II.F(1)(d)), for there is no mention of any quantitative energy analysis.
entities.stance_basis: identifies major deficiencies in energy analysis and requires additional information in final EIS

entities.role: Acting Assistant Director for Environmental Quality, Federal Power Commission
entities.quote: We note major deficiencies in your presentation of the impact of the proposed project on "Energy" (Section II.F(1)(d)), for there is no mention of any quantitative energy analysis.
entities.stance_basis: identifies major deficiencies in energy analysis and requires additional information in final EIS

entities.quote: The project should have a most beneficial impact upon downtown Atlanta as well as upon GSA activities.
entities.stance_basis: supports project as beneficial to downtown Atlanta and GSA activities

entities.role: Director, Environmental Affairs, General Services Administration
entities.quote: The project should have a most beneficial impact upon downtown Atlanta as well as upon GSA activities.
entities.stance_basis: supports project as beneficial to downtown Atlanta and GSA activities

entities.quote: We have reviewed the statement with regard to impacts on the Federal-aid highway system in the vicinity of the proposed action and have no adverse comments.
entities.stance_basis: no adverse comments but suggests coordination with ARC and use of RDP traffic projections in final EIS

entities.quote: It is suggested that the recommended transportation improvements (pages 220-225 and page 382) be coordinated with the Atlanta Regional Commission (ARC) concerning inclusion of suggested improvements in the Atlanta Metro Transportation Improvement Program (TIP)
entities.stance_basis: no adverse comments but conditions on coordination with ARC and use of updated traffic projections

entities.role: Staff Hydrologist, U.S. Department of the Interior, Water Resources Division
entities.quote: I have no comments to offer on the subject Draft Environmental Impact Statement.
entities.stance_basis: declines to comment, deferring to Office of Environmental Project Review

entities.role: Director, Environmental Project Review, U.S. Department of the Interior
entities.quote: This is to inform you that the Department will have comments but will be unable to reply within the allotted time as we have just received your transmittal. Please consider this letter as a request for an extension of time in which to comment on the statement.
entities.stance_basis: requests extension of time to comment, neither supporting nor opposing

entities.entity: United States Department of the Interior, Office of the Secretary
entities.quote: The statement does not adequately identify cultural resources, nor does it adequately assess the project's potential environmental impacts on these resources.
entities.stance_basis: identifies deficiencies in cultural resources analysis and requires additional studies before approval

entities.role: Special Assistant to the Secretary, U.S. Department of the Interior
entities.quote: The statement does not adequately identify cultural resources, nor does it adequately assess the project's potential environmental impacts on these resources.
entities.stance_basis: identifies deficiencies in cultural resources analysis and requires additional studies

entities.entity: Department of Health, Education, and Welfare Region IV
entities.quote: Based upon the data contained in the draft, it is our opinion that the proposed action will have only a minor impact upon the human environment within the scope of this Department's review. The impact statement has been adequately addressed for our comments.
entities.stance_basis: finds only minor impact and considers EIS adequately addressed

entities.role: Regional Environmental Officer, DHEW Region IV
entities.quote: Based upon the data contained in the draft, it is our opinion that the proposed action will have only a minor impact upon the human environment within the scope of this Department's review. The impact statement has been adequately addressed for our comments.
entities.stance_basis: finds only minor impact and considers EIS adequately addressed

entities.entity: Department of the Army, Mobile District, Corps of Engineers
entities.quote: The statement is considered adequate as related to the Corps of Engineers responsibilities. We have no specific comments.
entities.stance_basis: considers EIS adequate with no specific comments

entities.role: Chief, Planning Division, Department of the Army Mobile District Corps of Engineers
entities.quote: The statement is considered adequate as related to the Corps of Engineers responsibilities. We have no specific comments.
entities.stance_basis: considers EIS adequate with no specific comments

entities.entity: United States Department of Agriculture, Soil Conservation Service
entities.quote: As a result of this review, we find that all areas of our concern are adequately covered in this draft. Your agency is to be complimented on the quality of this draft EIS.
entities.stance_basis: finds all areas of concern adequately covered and compliments quality of draft EIS

entities.role: State Conservationist, USDA Soil Conservation Service
entities.quote: As a result of this review, we find that all areas of our concern are adequately covered in this draft. Your agency is to be complimented on the quality of this draft EIS.
entities.stance_basis: finds all areas of concern adequately covered and compliments quality of draft EIS

entities.quote: With respect to the above-referenced Draft Environmental Impact Statement, we would like to submit the following comments:
entities.stance_basis: submits multiple factual corrections and clarifications to the draft EIS on behalf of the redeveloper

entities.quote: This draft EIS is unbalanced and incomplete in that it concentrates on the 78 acre redevelopment proposal and on physical impacts. It is inadequate in its appraisal of the impacts of the Project Rehab program.
entities.stance_basis: finds draft EIS unbalanced, incomplete, and technically deficient in multiple respects
